what_it_does: Acceptance test script for the semantic-radius projection path (Group 6) and the strain metric (Group 7). It constructs synthetic corpus events and 2D unit vectors, calls runtime.projection.project with radius_from='semantic', and asserts that the centre maps to the origin, neighbour radii are monotone in cosine distance, the centre's cosine=1.0 outlier is excluded from the normalization band, vectorless points land on the rim with r_unknown, missing centre vectors raise ValueError, non-semantic bindings ignore vectors, and strain equals the gap between semantic and structural radius.
observations: Self-contained hermetic test using synthetic vectors, no live server or store.
Uses vec_at(cos) to build exact 2D unit vectors and keys them by run:// corpus addresses plus the centre.
Explicitly guards the empty-core regression: including the centre in normalization would push the nearest neighbour to ~0.39; the fix requires it near ~0.06.
A vectorless point X is expected at r=1.0 with r_unknown=True rather than being dropped.
Semantic radius fails loud when center is None or the centre has no vector in the space.
Includes Group 7 strain checks for centre/coherent (low), divergence (high), and far-in-both (low) cases.
standouts: The empty-core regression guard is the dominant signal: it documents and asserts a specific numerical normalization fix.
Strain is surfaced and tested as |r_struct - r|, with dispositive checks that the centre has strain ~0.
Fail-loud design: semantic radius requires a centre vector and never silently falls back to time-based radius.
conventions: Flat script style with global PASS/FAIL counters and a helper check function.
Inline comments serve as design rationale and bug history.
Imports runtime.projection.project and BindingRegistry; adds parent directory via sys.path.
Uses ISO timestamps and timedelta for deterministic event ages.
concerns: Global mutable PASS/FAIL counters and broad try/except blocks for error assertions.
Hard-coded numeric thresholds (e.g., rN1 < 0.15, strain > 0.5) are not derived from fixture parameters.
Depends on internal projection output fields (radius_normalized, r_unknown, r_struct, strain) that may not be a stable public contract.
No test isolation or cleanup; the script mutates global state.
notes: Hermetic acceptance test that encodes a specific semantic-radius normalization fix and a structural-vs-semantic strain metric in one runnable script.
questions: What exact normalization formula does runtime.projection.project use, and is the centre always excluded or only for semantic radius?
How is r_struct computed, and does structural radius use the same [0,1] banding?
What happens when multiple points share the same cosine or all non-centre vectors are identical?
Is the strain field intended to be persisted or only surfaced in projection output?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose matches the implementation: it guards semantic-radius projection and strain behavior with explicit regression checks.
apparent_themes: Coherence vs divergence between semantic and structural position.
intention_signals: The file is trying to lock in a corrected normalization for semantic projection so the centre does not swallow the radius budget, and to expose structural/semantic disagreement as a first-class 'strain' signal.
novelty: It treats projection radius as a normalized meaning-distance with an explicit exclusion rule for the centre outlier, and couples it with a strain metric derived from structural-semantic disagreement.
contribution: A runnable, deterministic acceptance contract for semantic-radius projection and strain, capturing expected edge behaviors (empty-core fix, vectorless rim flag, fail-loud missing centre, additive non-semantic binding) that a fused system can use as regression protection.
summary_for_embedding: Hermetic acceptance test for runtime.projection.project's semantic-radius path and strain metric, using synthetic vectors to assert centre-at-origin, monotone full-radius normalization excluding the centre outlier, vectorless points at the rim, fail-loud missing centre vectors, and structural-semantic divergence as strain.
intention_for_embedding: To guard the semantic-radius projection fix and the strain divergence metric against regression with deterministic synthetic fixtures.

symbols.does: Test assertion helper that increments PASS/FAIL counters and prints a pass/fail line.
symbols.description: A simple inline assertion reporter used throughout the script; it mutates global PASS and FAIL and prints emoji-prefixed pass/fail lines with optional detail.

symbols.does: Returns a 2D unit vector with a specified cosine against [1,0].
symbols.description: Constructs deterministic synthetic embedding coordinates so the test can dial exact cosines and therefore exact semantic distances without needing a live embedding model.

symbols.does: Builds a corpus.record-shaped event for a given sequence, source address, and age.
symbols.description: Creates a synthetic event dictionary with seq, kind, timestamp, run:// address, source_address, and summary, used as input fixtures for project().

symbols.does: Callable that invokes project() with center=None for the semantic binding.
symbols.description: Encapsulates the call expected to raise ValueError because semantic radius requires a centre to measure meaning-distance from; used in a try/except guard.

symbols.does: Callable that invokes project() with a centre that has no vector in the space.
symbols.description: Encapsulates the call expected to raise ValueError because the semantic path refuses to fabricate a radius when the centre lacks an embedding.
